SAP Ariba system maintenance

SAP Ariba will be unavailable for scheduled maintenance at the times listed below. We apologize for any inconvenience.

Saturday, March 21 from 8:00 pm until 11:59 pm (EDT)

Buy Canadian Policy

The Government of Canada has introduced new policies to protect, build, and transform Canadian industries. Check out Buy Canadian Policy for more information on what this could mean for you.

Welcome to the new Buyer’s Portal!

As of January 30, 2026, the Buyer’s Portal is the official source of information for the Acquisitions Program.

Please note that the PSPC Supply Manual has been archived and is no longer being maintained.

Explore the Buyer's Portal and send us your comments .

Interim Policy on Reciprocal Procurement

Canada implemented the Interim Policy on Reciprocal Procurement to protect and support Canadian workers and businesses and to develop mutually beneficial and resilient supply chains.

Under the Policy, Canadian businesses have default access to all federal procurement opportunities. Foreign businesses are only eligible for procurements that are subject to a trade agreement between Canada and the supplier’s jurisdiction.

The Policy applies to new procurements (excluding defence procurements) and renewals of supply arrangements or standing offers, but not existing contracts.

The Policy came into effect on July 14, 2025.

For more information on the steps to apply the Policy, see the Buyer's Guide, Interim Policy on Reciprocal Procurement .
